metadata.title: Start a Vending Business
metadata.seoTitle: How to Start a Vending Business: A Step-by-Step Guide
metadata.metaDescription: Learn how to start a vending business. Discover key demand signals like foot traffic and dwell time to validate your location before you buy machines.

props.heading: Start Your Vending Machine Business
props.body: Build a sustainable income stream by identifying high-demand locations first.

props.heading: The Golden Rule: Validate Before You Buy

nodes.text: Before purchasing any equipment, new vending operators must validate location demand. Buying machines without a confirmed, high-traffic spot is a common mistake for beginners.
Look for these three signals to determine if a location is viable for your vending business:

items: High Foot Traffic: The sheer number of people passing by the machine.
Dwell Time: How long people stay in the vicinity of the machine.
Existing Food Options: Lack of nearby snacks or drinks increases demand.

items.question: When should I buy my first vending machine?
items.answer: You should only purchase machines after you have validated the demand at your target location. Confirming foot traffic and dwell time ensures your investment is backed by real data.

warnings.message: The source material only covers location validation. Additional content regarding legal setup, machine types, and maintenance requires more source documentation.
